If you use your own custom [`baseQuery`](../../rtk-query/usage/customizing-queries), you can customize this behavior to your liking.

old_column.content: * Unlike the `createApi` version which can _only_ return a string, this per-endpoint option can also return an an object, number, or boolean.  If it returns a string, that value will be used as the cache key directly.  If it returns an object / number / boolean, that value will be passed to the built-in `defaultSerializeQueryArgs`.  This simplifies the use case of stripping out args you don't want included in the cache key.
